zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Woodhouse於大約1490年出生在英國諾福爾可吉波利時，他的父親Sir Thomas Woodhouse14歲，母親Thomasine Townsend16歲。他和Elizabeth Radcliffe至少有6個兒子和9個女兒。他去世於1560年2月10日之前，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可瓦可哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Woodhouse于大约1490年出生在英国諾福爾可吉波利时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Woodhouse14岁，母亲Thomasine Townsend16岁。他和Elizabeth Radcliffe至少有6个儿子和9个女儿。他去世于1560年2月10日之前，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可瓦可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Woodhouse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of various places (in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Yorkshire, Northumberland, Shropshire, and elsewhere) called Woodhouse, or a topographic name for someone who lived at a ‘house in the wood’ (Middle English wode, hous, Old English wudu, hūs).

variant of Woodiwiss , from Middle English wodewose, which by the 16th century was sometimes written as wodowes, woodose, and wodehouse. The confusion with woodhouse probably arose because both words (and both names) were pronounced /wudus/ or /wudǝs/ in local dialect.

variants.name: Woodis
meanings: perhaps a nickname from Old English wuduwāsa‘faun, satyr’.
alternatively, a shortened form of   Woodhouse  .

variants.name: Woodiwiss
meanings: English (mainly Derbyshire and Yorkshire): nickname from Middle English wodewose, wodewese (Old English wudewāse) ‘wild man of the woods’. This word was also used to denote a person dressed to represent such a being in a play or pageant. Wodewoses (male and female) were mythical creatures of English and ancient Germanic folklore, akin to the fauns and satyrs of Classical mythology. The surname may also have been acquired by someone who lived and worked in the woods or perhaps by an unusually hairy man.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Woodhouse was born about 1490, in Kimberley, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir Thomas Woodhouse, was 14 and his mother, Thomasine Townsend, was 16. He had at least 6 sons and 9 daughters with Elizabeth Radcliffe. He died before 10 February 1560, and was buried in Waxham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Woodhouse est né environ 1490, à Kimberley, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Woodhouse, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Thomasine Townsend, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 9 filles avec Elizabeth Radcliffe. Il est décédé avant 10 février 1560, et a été enterré à Waxham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Woodhouse um 1490 in Kimberley, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas Woodhouse 14 und seine Mutter Thomasine Townsend 16. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Radcliffe mindestens 6 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Er starb vor 10. Februar 1560 und wurde in Waxham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Woodhouse è nato il circa 1490 nel Kimberley, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Thomas Woodhouse, aveva 14 anni e il suo madre, Thomasine Townsend, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 9 figlie con Elizabeth Radcliffe. Morì prima del 10 febbraio 1560, e fu sepolto a Waxham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Woodhouseが約1490年にイギリスノーフォルクキンバーリーで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Woodhouseは14歳，母Thomasine Townsendは16歳であった。彼はElizabeth Radcliffeとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と9人の娘をもうけた。彼は1560年2月10日以前に死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクワクスハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Woodhouse(이)가 대략 1490년 영국 노르폴크 킴벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Woodhouse(은)는 14세였고, 그의 어머니 Thomasine Townsend(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Radcliffe(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1560년 2월 10일 이전 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 와스함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Woodhouse nasceu aproximadamente 1490, em Kimberley, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Woodhouse, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Thomasine Townsend, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 9 filhas com Elizabeth Radcliffe. Ele faleceu antes 10 de fevereiro de 1560, e foi sepultado em Waxham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Woodhouse родился около 1490 г. в местности кимбирли, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Woodhouse было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Thomasine Townsend, 16. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 9 дочерей от Elizabeth Radcliffe. Он умер перед 10 февраль 1560 г. и был похоронен в местности ваксхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Woodhouse nació aproximadamente 1490, en Kimberley, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Woodhouse, tenía 14 años y su mamá, Thomasine Townsend, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 9 hijas con Elizabeth Radcliffe. Murió antes de 10 de febrero de 1560, y fue sepultado en Waxham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
